codeReview.summary: Reviewed handoff, transcript skeleton, skill guidance, and commit 790e5ac1f0d493f809ad45b888247313016a8bb4. Changes align with the feature scope (docs/config/dependency polish plus noUnused cleanup), and no high-confidence functional defects were found in the attributable diff.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Task wording referenced 'AGENTS.md (repo root)' while the active operational AGENTS file is mission-scoped. The worker transcript shows attempts against both paths, which indicates ambiguity.
sharedStateObservations.suggestedChange: In mission AGENTS guidance, explicitly state whether AGENTS updates for polish tasks should be made in mission AGENTS only, and note if no repo-root AGENTS.md exists.
sharedStateObservations.rationale: Reduces path confusion and prevents future workers from probing/editing the wrong AGENTS location.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton for workerSessionId a37e95a9-d579-4eff-9fe6-eecae3fa58fc includes reads of both /web-demo-v2/AGENTS.md and /Users/plebdev/.factory/missions/.../AGENTS.md; repo root listing currently has no AGENTS.md.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Feature was assigned runtime-worker even though the work was primarily docs/config/dependency polish. Handoff recorded followedProcedure=false solely due skipping agent-browser.
sharedStateObservations.suggestedChange: Add planner/skill guidance that docs/config-only polish features should use a non-runtime skill (or explicitly waive agent-browser when runtime-worker is used for non-UI docs/config tasks).
sharedStateObservations.rationale: Avoids avoidable procedure-deviation noise and keeps skill compliance signals meaningful.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json sets skillName=runtime-worker for polish-2nd-pass-docs-config-deps; handoff.skillFeedback marks followedProcedure=false with the only deviation being agent-browser omission.

summary: Pass. The feature’s commit-level implementation is consistent with the requested docs/config/dependency polish and includes the expected cleanup/config updates without introducing high-confidence actionable defects.
